name: Lynette's Opal
original_description:
A personal favourite. As expected we got some very fun, fluid and soft glazes.

Looks pretty much the exact same in cone 5 as it does 6.
input_for_ai:
유약 이름은 Lynette's Opal입니다. 현재 Testing 단계이며, 상세 설명은 다음과 같습니다: A personal favourite. As expected we got some very fun, fluid and soft glazes.

Looks pretty much the exact same in cone 5 as it does 6.

name: OldForge FirstFive Floating Base
original_description:
This is one of a selection of straightforward recipes that use the same 5 base ingredients, to give an easy starting point to buying ingredients and mixing glazes: https://www.oldforgecreations.co.uk/blog/first-five-ingredients-where-to-start-with-glaze-making

This is a fluid base with titanium, which should produce flowing variegated glazes with added colourants and/or in combination. All colourant tests shown are in addition to the titanium. If you're not getting the phase separation and opacity, try a thicker application, a cooler firing, or adding a little more titanium.

If you use an immersion blender to mix your tests, you will need to use the same blender for the full batches, as it can have [a drastic impact on the result with certain colourants (including titanium)](https://glazy.org/posts/173081)

I'm separating out the colour tests as I do them on larger pieces to make them easier to find:

[Original](https://glazy.org/materials/144590)
[Cobalt](https://glazy.org/recipes/150980)
[Copper](https://glazy.org/materials/150981)
[Chrome](https://glazy.org/materials/219390)
[Rutile](https://glazy.org/recipes/236647)
input_for_ai:
유약 이름은 OldForge FirstFive Floating Base입니다. 현재 Testing 단계이며, 상세 설명은 다음과 같습니다: This is one of a selection of straightforward recipes that use the same 5 base ingredients, to give an easy starting point to buying ingredients and mixing glazes: https://www.oldforgecreations.co.uk/blog/first-five-ingredients-where-to-start-with-glaze-making

This is a fluid base with titanium, which should produce flowing variegated glazes with added colourants and/or in combination. All colourant tests shown are in addition to the titanium. If you're not getting the phase separation and opacity, try a thicker application, a cooler firing, or adding a little more titanium.

If you use an immersion blender to mix your tests, you will need to use the same blender for the full batches, as it can have [a drastic impact on the result with certain colourants (including titanium)](https://glazy.org/posts/173081)

I'm separating out the colour tests as I do them on larger pieces to make them easier to find:

[Original](https://glazy.org/materials/144590)
[Cobalt](https://glazy.org/recipes/150980)
[Copper](https://glazy.org/materials/150981)
[Chrome](https://glazy.org/materials/219390)
[Rutile](https://glazy.org/recipes/236647)

name: Dune
original_description:
A well behaved, easy to apply matte with texture like a soft polished stone.

Adjustments made to the original recipe:
- Reduced Nickel Oxide to 1 to create a more neutral tone.
- Added more Neph Sye to balance the R2O:RO ratio to reduce leaching.

Tested for leaching and crazing, so far, so good on the listed Standard Ceramic mid-fire clays. Please do your own testing as well to ensure food safety!

See also Will Donovan's Eggshell Satin - a similar recipe that I wasn't aware of when altering this one: https://glazy.org/recipes/246036
input_for_ai:
유약 이름은 Dune입니다. 현재 Production 단계이며, 상세 설명은 다음과 같습니다: A well behaved, easy to apply matte with texture like a soft polished stone.

Adjustments made to the original recipe:
- Reduced Nickel Oxide to 1 to create a more neutral tone.
- Added more Neph Sye to balance the R2O:RO ratio to reduce leaching.

Tested for leaching and crazing, so far, so good on the listed Standard Ceramic mid-fire clays. Please do your own testing as well to ensure food safety!

See also Will Donovan's Eggshell Satin - a similar recipe that I wasn't aware of when altering this one: https://glazy.org/recipes/246036

name: Crystal
original_description:
This glaze is applied thinly to the test tiles, fired at a 45deg. angle, and pots shown.  It will not run, as attested to by the photos, if put on very thin.  I used a spray gun with four very quick passes.
Water= 60 per 100 batch

Success with this glaze depends on how well your kiln is dialed in.
Firing schedule:
Ramp 1 500f 2094f Hold 5min
Ramp 2 300f 2294f Hold 10min
Ramp 3 9999 1963f hold 360min

Adjust the kiln ramps and temp to suit how hot or cold your kiln fires.  To hot=few to no crystals.  To cold=the glazes is over nucleated and appears matte.
input_for_ai:
유약 이름은 Crystal입니다. 현재 Production 단계이며, 상세 설명은 다음과 같습니다: This glaze is applied thinly to the test tiles, fired at a 45deg. angle, and pots shown.  It will not run, as attested to by the photos, if put on very thin.  I used a spray gun with four very quick passes.
Water= 60 per 100 batch

Success with this glaze depends on how well your kiln is dialed in.
Firing schedule:
Ramp 1 500f 2094f Hold 5min
Ramp 2 300f 2294f Hold 10min
Ramp 3 9999 1963f hold 360min

Adjust the kiln ramps and temp to suit how hot or cold your kiln fires.  To hot=few to no crystals.  To cold=the glazes is over nucleated and appears matte.

name: Akiko's crater glaze
original_description: This is a base recipe for Volcanic glazes given to me by my teacher Akiko Hirai. She recommends using it on stoneware with a black slip underneath but it responds well to other additives ie 2-3% copper oxide turns it turquoise and on porcelain it looks like coral encrustations.
input_for_ai: 유약 이름은 Akiko's crater glaze입니다. 현재 Production 단계이며, 상세 설명은 다음과 같습니다: This is a base recipe for Volcanic glazes given to me by my teacher Akiko Hirai. She recommends using it on stoneware with a black slip underneath but it responds well to other additives ie 2-3% copper oxide turns it turquoise and on porcelain it looks like coral encrustations.

name: Keen Green
original_description:
Tennessee Technological University - Appalachian Center for Crafts - Clay Studio
￼
Vince Pitelka, 2015

Most of the glaze recipes below are given here as they were provided to us by Susan Demay.

https://sites.tntech.edu/wpitelka/handoutsinformation/

Original Recipe:

Gerstley Borate 1360 
Potash Spar 4600 
Flint 1880 
EPK 250 
Whiting 810 
Zircopax 600 
Dolomite 600 
Bentonite 100 
Copper Carb 663 
Rutile 250

name: Studio White
original_description:
https://mightymudmixer.com/shop/studio-white/

This mixture is a very forgiving white glaze, creamy and brilliant… Will accept underglazing applied over to produce a type of Majolica effect.
input_for_ai:
유약 이름은 Studio White입니다. 현재 Production 단계이며, 상세 설명은 다음과 같습니다: https://mightymudmixer.com/shop/studio-white/
